GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "This heritage jewellery brand brings French style to the everyday", "With a sense of timelessness that feels inherently modern, FRED is the French maison you need to know.", "There's no better feeling than finding the perfect piece of jewellery. Whether it's a gemstone-filled ring which takes your fancy or a gorgeous gold pair of earrings, as any lover of jewels knows, the thrill of the find is all part of the fun. Which is why a browse at Fred, the heritage French maison known for contemporary yet creative pieces, ticks all the boxes. Founded by Fred Samuel, a French jeweller who grew up in Argentina, in the 1930s, the eponymous brand is interlinked with some of the greatest moments and people of the last century. From the Monaco royal family of whom FRED was the official jeweller (Princess Grace of Monaco opened his store in Monte Carlo) to features in Hollywood blockbusters, FRED has been everywhere and seen it all. However, Samuel's aesthetic was never to dwell on history. Quite the opposite, in fact, with the jeweller proudly stating "objects from the past bore me". "I would never even think of taking inspiration from a traditional or vintage piece of jewellery, or ever even shop for antique pieces with the intention of imitating one I might find fascinating. Antique jewellery should stay in museums," he can be quoted as saying. Rising to fame in the 70s after a post-oil boom in clients from the Middle East, Samuel and the FRED brand became known for its love of coloured gemstones. It's a love that stretches back to the 50s when the Nepalese royal family asked Samuel to design jewels to match the saris worn in court. Inspired by the local silks, he produced pieces packed with sapphires and a love of the coloured stone began. As Samuel himself stated, "I learned about love from these gems that both absorb and emit light." Further, in 1977, the jeweller introduced the Soleil d'Or in a yellow, 105-carat colourway, which was widely considered to be one of the most extraordinary diamonds in the world. Nowadays, the brand is best known for two main collections, Force 10 and the more recent Chance Infinie. Force 10, which is deeply inspired by the element of water, was crafted in 1966 by Samuel's eldest son for his wife's birthday. A sports-inspired collection for both men and women, even Force 10's name evokes a tale of the sea, with the classification relating to the strongest type of gale force wind. "It was my eldest son's idea to design a bracelet for his wife with braided sailing cables that would be riveted on either end. He added a gold clasp in the shape of a carabiner," Samuel noted of the collection, which would then go on to become emblematic of the brand as a whole. Chance Infinie, which launched in 2016 to commemorate 80 years of FRED, is one of the brand's more contemporary collections. The latest iteration was the vision of artistic director Valerie Samuel, who enlisted South African-born designer Annelise Michelson to create a capsule that was equal parts sensual, feminine and sculpture - hallmarks of modern style. The resulting pieces are inherently fluid, wearable and incredibly beautiful. An array of silver, gold and rose gold pieces, each Chance Infinie iteration harks back to the hallmarks of the brand - including colour and form - with a modern substance. Now, for those who like the idea of owning a piece of history - and royally-approved history, at that - FRED is available at their boutique located in Sydney. Visit FRED's website to view the further range."
